title: prunus avium var. regalis
intro: Prunus avium var. regalis, also known as Royal Cherry, is a variety of wild cherry tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its dark purple fruit and fragrant white flowers. The bark of the Royal Cherry tree contains a compound called prunasin, which has been found to have medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of the Royal Cherry include its potential to help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Royal Cherry can be prepared as an herbal remedy by making a tea or tincture from the bark, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
